children.text: Saugatuck sits at the intersection of Westport’s commuter convenience and its village lifestyle. It has a slightly more connected, transit-forward feel than some of Westport’s more purely residential sections, and many buyers like that it’s easy to get in and out without giving up the Westport address.

Real estate in Saugatuck spans a range—from low-maintenance options like condos and townhomes to nearby single-family streets. In my experience, it often fits clients who want Westport’s lifestyle but don’t necessarily want the responsibility of a larger property or a longer drive to the train.

Westport station on the Metro-North New Haven Line is the key amenity. The MTA notes ticket machines on both platforms (two on the westbound platform and one on the eastbound near the elevator) and no ticket office, plus a waiting area and restrooms (daily 4 a.m.–12 noon). With downtown Westport close by, Saugatuck is best for buyers who want commuter efficiency and village access in one place.

highlights: Westport Metro-North station (New Haven Line) as a major commuter anchor
A transit-forward Westport pocket with convenient access to downtown
A range of housing options including lower-maintenance living near the train
Easy access to Main Street dining, shopping, and shoreline amenities
Vibrant neighborhood bridging downtown convenience and waterfront access
name: Saugatuck
overview: Saugatuck is Westport’s river-and-train neighborhood—walkable in pockets, connected to downtown, and especially convenient for commuters. Housing ranges from condos and townhomes to nearby single-family streets, with a lifestyle that balances village access and easy transit. The Westport Metro-North station is a major anchor, and downtown Westport is close when you want Main Street dining and shopping. If you want Westport with maximum commuter practicality, Saugatuck is a standout.
